name: Chicken Chunks with Peanuts in Spicy Sauce
recipeIngredient: 4 Green Onions, trimmed and cut into 1-inch sections
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the ends off green onions and cut into 1-inch sections. Mix all sauce ingredients in a 2-quart saucepan.
2. Reserve the sauce mixture.
3. Remove skin from chicken breasts, then separate meat from bones. Slice meat into 1-inch strips, then crosswise to make 1-inch chunks.
4. In a large bowl, mix egg white with water chestnut flour. Beat the mixture using a single chopstick (not an egg beater or whisk).
5. Stir in the chicken pieces and coat thoroughly. Marinate for 5 minutes.
6. Heat peanut oil in a wok or deep fryer to medium heat. Fry peanuts until lightly browned; if they brown too quickly, reduce the heat.
7. Remove the fried peanuts with a strainer or slotted spoon and drain on paper towel or paper bag.
8. Increase the heat slightly for chicken. Test by frying a piece of chicken: it should rise to the surface immediately and become golden brown in about 2 minutes.
9. Fry coated chicken chunks until golden brown, no more than 8 pieces at a time. Use long chopsticks or a spatula to keep pieces separate while frying.
10. Remove fried chicken with a slotted spoon.
11. While frying chicken, heat the sauce to simmer. Add green onions and peanuts about a minute before serving.
12. At the last moment, add the fried chicken pieces into the sauce, mix quickly, and serve.
